baseline.description: Current strict promoted TP4 local-argmax baseline
baseline.quality: Passed raw145 n64/n256 exact-token gates, semantic suite, arithmetic repeat, and extended sixpack.

result.firstFailure: piecewise_backend assertion: Expected exactly one compiled range_entry for static shape compilation, but found 2
result.withAutoCompileRangesDisabled: startup passed the assertion but failed loading weights with XPU OOM while allocating 144 MiB on GPU 2
result.decision: Do not promote. The GPU ngram proposer currently consumes enough extra compile/runtime memory to break this 4x32GB configuration.

result.asyncScheduling: vLLM disables async scheduling for ngram-based speculative decoding.
result.localArgmax: Logs show XPU local argmax is disabled once speculative metadata is present.
result.rerunFailure: Later json-fix rerun hit level_zero UR_RESULT_ERROR_OUT_OF_RESOURCES during compiled attention/KV initialization.
result.decision: Do not promote. Even when it runs, CPU ngram sacrifices the local-argmax fast path and is an order of magnitude slower than baseline.
Do not promote. XCCL all-reduce variants for this argmax path are still unsafe on this stack.

harnessChanges.change: Added --speculative-config passthrough and records a JSON-safe copy of the user-provided speculative config.

nextCandidates: Avoid stock speculative decoding unless a future patch keeps greedy verification on the local-argmax path.
Treat XCCL ReduceOp-based argmax collectives as unsafe until a standalone correctness probe proves otherwise.
Next promising direction is a custom deterministic XPU pair-reduction or eliminating the post-lm-head collective from the decode loop.
